column1: # apparatus_manifest.tsv -- the manifest that closes the apparatus-vs-description drift class.
# WHY: circle 122's Gate-A pass found the manuscript had gone coherent-and-wrong about its OWN
# apparatus -- it described a 4-member reflexive-monitor family while circles 119-121 had wired
# three more gates. That is a class NO prior gate covers: claim_consistency.py is prose-vs-prose,
# editorial_gate.py is prose-vs-evidence; this is CODE-vs-prose drift (the tooling changed, the
#   1. every script wired into execute_squeeze STEPS is classified here (a NEW gate added to the
#      squeeze forces a described/internal decision -- the drift cannot enter silently);
#   2. every `described` script is \code-referenced in tex/paper.tex AND exists on disk
#      (a described gate missing from the manuscript = drift = hard fail);
#   3. every verify/*.py the manuscript \code-references exists on disk and is listed here
#   described -- part of the manuscript's self-described apparatus; paper.tex MUST name it.
#   internal  -- object-level mechanics or a paper-impl writing principle; not in the paper's
